text: acute renal failure observed following contrast agent exposure
severe bradycardia developed after beta-blocker initiation
drug-induced hemolytic anemia confirmed in susceptible patient
the immunosuppressant caused opportunistic cytomegalovirus infection
cutaneous vasculitis reported after allopurinol therapy
ventricular tachycardia precipitated by the antiarrhythmic agent
ototoxicity with permanent hearing loss following aminoglycoside therapy
transaminase elevations resolved after discontinuation of the drug
no treatment-emergent adverse events were reported in this arm
the study drug was safe and well tolerated across the age spectrum
all efficacy endpoints were met with no major safety concerns
the compound showed no evidence of nephrotoxicity in renal studies
